name: node-jsesc
title: node-jsesc
summary: Escape JavaScript strings, with short output (Node.js module)
description:
Escape JavaScript strings, with short output (Node.js module)
Escapes JavaScript strings while generating the shortes possible valid
ASCII-only output. This can be used to avoid various encoding issues.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.

commands.name: jsesc
commands.description: escape strings for use in JavaScript string literals

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use jsesc because Escape JavaScript strings, with short output (Node.js module).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use jsesc as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `jsesc [-s | --single-quotes string]` as documented upstream for jsesc.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `jsesc 'foo bar baz'` as documented upstream for jsesc.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair jsesc with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `jsesc --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: jsesc -- escape strings for use in JavaScript string literals
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install node-jsesc. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `jsesc [-s | --single-quotes string]`. Escape JavaScript strings, with short output (Node.js module).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Escape JavaScript strings, with short output (Node.js module).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: jsesc -- escape strings for use in JavaScript string literals.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install node-jsesc
Install with: sudo apt install node-jsesc
